hero.question: Israel x Hamas Ceasefire Phase II by June 30?
hero.prob_take: The crowd is pricing a Phase II ceasefire by June 30 at just 20.5%, which assumes negotiations collapse or drag past the deadline, despite both sides having already agreed to Phase I framework and ceasefire mechanics. That discount looks too steep given the political pressure on both Netanyahu and Hamas to show progress within 6 months, especially with Phase I serving as a template and reducing negotiation friction.

daily_take.headline: Gaza Ceasefire Odds Crash. The Market Just Priced in Reality.
daily_take.deck: Israel x Hamas Phase II ceasefire odds dropped 3 points to 20.5% overnight, signaling fresh skepticism about near-term peace talks. The market is right. With negotiators deadlocked on hostage releases and territorial withdrawals, expecting a deal by June 30 requires believing both sides suddenly compromise when they haven't budged in months. Smart money stays out or fades any rallies on optimistic headlines.
